heading: Johnstown vs Jollys Lookout — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Johnstown, Queensland and Jollys Lookout, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jollys Lookout has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Johnstown (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Johnstown or Jollys Lookout safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Johnstown has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Jollys Lookout scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Johnstown scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Jollys Lookout scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Johnstown or Jollys Lookout?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jollys Lookout has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
